title: Problem Trending
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident"
| stats count by category, subcategory, cmdb_ci
| where count > 5
| sort -count
| head 20
description: Identifying recurring incident patterns that should become problems drives root cause resolution and reduces incident volume.
value: Identifying recurring incident patterns that should become problems drives root cause resolution and reduces incident volume.
implementation: Analyze incident patterns by category, CI, and assignment group. Identify recurring incidents (>5 in 30 days). Flag candidates for problem record creation. Track problem management effectiveness (repeat incidents after RCA).
visualization: Table (top recurring incidents), Bar chart (repeat incidents by category), Line chart (repeat rate trend).
knownFalsePositives: Problem work rising during big outages, vendor bugs, and right after a major product release; context matters for trend pages.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Incident categorization data, problem records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Analyze incident patterns by category, CI, and assignment group. Identify recurring incidents (>5 in 30 days). Flag candidates for problem record creation. Track problem management effectiveness (repeat incidents after RCA).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident"
| stats count by category, subcategory, cmdb_ci
| where count > 5
| sort -count
| head 20
```

Understanding this SPL

**Problem Trending** — Identifying recurring incident patterns that should become problems drives root cause resolution and reduces incident volume.

Documented **Data sources**: Incident categorization data, problem records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:incident". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by category, subcategory, cmdb_ci** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count > 5` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow configuration item or related CMDB view. Spot-check a few CIs, owners, and relationship fields, then confirm discovery dates if we measure freshness.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (top recurring incidents), Bar chart (repeat incidents by category), Line chart (repeat rate trend).
grandmaExplanation: We watch how many underlying problems are open and moving, so we can fund root-cause work before the same pain keeps coming back.
